title: How to Use Global Sections in GoHighLevel — Save Time Building
description: Learn how to use Global Sections in GoHighLevel to save time building pages, funnels & websites. Step-by-step guide + real-world use cases.
html_content:
Building landing pages, sales funnels, and websites in GoHighLevel doesn't have to mean starting from scratch every single time.

If you're managing multiple client campaigns or running your own business, you've probably felt the drag of recreating the same header, footer, testimonial section, or call-to-action block over and over. That's where Global Sections come in—and they're one of the most underrated time-savers in the platform.

Global Sections let you build once, use everywhere. Create a section (header, hero, pricing table, footer, whatever), save it globally, and instantly deploy it across unlimited pages. When you update that section, it updates across every page that uses it. No manual edits. No inconsistency. Just speed.

In this guide, I'll walk you through exactly how to leverage Global Sections to cut your build time in half—and show you real-world examples of where they'll save you the most time. If you're serious about scaling your business with GoHighLevel, you need to master this feature. Plus, if you're ready to go deeper into the platform, the GoHighLevel Bootcamp covers this and advanced strategies for agencies and business owners.

📋 In This Guide

  • What Are Global Sections in GoHighLevel?
  • How to Create and Save a Global Section
  • How to Use Saved Global Sections Across Your Pages
  • How to Edit and Update Global Sections
  • Real-World Use Cases for Global Sections
  • Best Practices for Organizing Global Sections

What Are Global Sections in GoHighLevel?

A Global Section is a reusable, page-building component that you create once and deploy across multiple pages, funnels, websites, and campaigns. Unlike regular sections that only exist on a single page, Global Sections are stored in your account and can be applied to any page you build.

The magic happens when you update a Global Section. Every page using that section automatically reflects the change—no manual updates needed. This is especially powerful if you're managing multiple clients or running campaigns where consistency matters.

Global Sections vs. Regular Sections:

  • Regular Sections: Live on a single page. Changes only affect that page.
  • Global Sections: Stored in your library. Changes apply to every page using them.

Think of Global Sections as templates. You design once, then reuse infinitely across your business.

💡 Pro Tip

Global Sections are ideal for elements that appear frequently across your digital presence: branded headers, footers, opt-in forms, testimonial sliders, pricing tables, and CTAs. The more you standardize, the more time you save.

How to Create and Save a Global Section

Creating a Global Section in GoHighLevel is straightforward. Follow these steps:

Step 1: Navigate to Your Page Builder

Open the page, funnel, or website you're building in GoHighLevel. Go to the page builder where you can see your canvas and design elements on the right sidebar.

Step 2: Choose Your Section Layout

At the top of the right sidebar, click Section. A list of pre-designed layout options will appear. Drag and drop the section you want into your builder canvas. You can also customize a blank section by adding text, images, buttons, and other elements.

Step 3: Customize Your Section

Design your section exactly as you want it to appear. Add colors, fonts, images, buttons, forms—whatever you need. This is your template, so make it polished and on-brand.

Step 4: Save as Global Section

Once your section is perfect, right-click on the section in the canvas or look for the section menu options. Select Save as Global Section. You'll be prompted to name it (e.g., "Header – Main Brand," "Footer – Company Info," "CTA – Free Consultation").

Step 5: Confirm and Store

GoHighLevel saves your Global Section to your library. It's now available on any page you build moving forward.

That's it. You've just created your first Global Section.

How to Use Saved Global Sections Across Your Pages

Once you've created a Global Section, using it is even faster than creating it.

Method 1: From the Section Menu

  1. Open any page in your builder.
  2. Click Section at the top of the right sidebar.
  3. Scroll down past the pre-designed layouts. You'll see a Global Sections or Saved Sections category.
  4. Click on the Global Section you want to add.
  5. Drag it onto your canvas. It instantly applies with all its design and functionality intact.

Method 2: Using the Search Feature (New)

GoHighLevel recently added a search feature that makes finding saved Global Sections even faster. At the top of the Section menu, type the name of your Global Section (e.g., "header" or "footer"). The search narrows down your options instantly. This is a game-changer if you have dozens of Global Sections saved.

Method 3: Duplicate and Modify

If you want to use a Global Section but need minor tweaks for a specific page, you can duplicate it, convert it to a regular section, and edit it independently. The original Global Section remains unchanged.

💡 Pro Tip

Use descriptive names for your Global Sections. Instead of "Section 1," name it "Header – Main Brand – Logo Left" or "CTA – Book Demo – Blue Button." Future you (and any team members) will appreciate the clarity.

How to Edit and Update Global Sections

One of the biggest advantages of Global Sections is centralized editing. When you update a Global Section, every page using it reflects those changes automatically.

To Edit a Global Section:

  1. Open any page that contains the Global Section you want to edit.
  2. Click on the Global Section in your canvas.
  3. Make your changes (edit text, swap images, change colors, etc.).
  4. A notification appears asking: "Apply changes to Global Section?" Click Yes.
  5. GoHighLevel pushes the update across every page using that section.

Alternative: Edit from the Global Section Library

Some versions of GoHighLevel allow you to access your Global Section library directly from your account settings. You can preview, edit, or delete Global Sections without being on a specific page.

Important Note: If you only want to change a section on one specific page without affecting the global version, convert it to a regular section first, then edit. This breaks the link to the Global Section and lets you customize independently.

Real-World Use Cases for Global Sections

Here's where Global Sections deliver the most ROI:

1. Branded Headers and Footers Your header likely has your logo, navigation, and contact info on every page. Create one Global Header Section, drop it on all 20 pages of your website, and maintain consistency instantly. If you rebrand, one edit updates everything.

2. Testimonial Carousels Build a testimonial slider section once with your best client reviews, videos, and ratings. Drop it on your homepage, sales page, and service pages. Every visitor sees the same powerful social proof.

3. Call-to-Action Blocks Standardize your CTAs across funnels. Build a "Book a Demo" or "Get Free Audit" section, save it globally, and deploy it across landing pages, webinar signup pages, and thank-you pages. Consistency drives higher conversion rates.

4. Pricing Tables If you have multiple pricing pages or need to show pricing in different contexts, a Global Pricing Table Section ensures consistency. Update prices once, and they reflect everywhere instantly.

5. FAQ Sections Build a comprehensive FAQ section and reuse it across service pages. When you add new FAQs, they appear on every page using that Global Section.

6. Multi-Client Builds Managing pages for 10 clients? Create client-specific header/footer Global Sections. Launch new pages faster and maintain brand consistency per client.

Best Practices for Organizing Global Sections

1. Use a Clear Naming Convention Organize by category and page location. Examples:

  • "Header – Main Navigation"
  • "Footer – Multi-Column – Gray"
  • "CTA – Book Demo – Blue"
  • "Testimonial – 3-Column Slider"
  • "Hero – Service Page – Large Image"

2. Avoid Over-Globalization Not every section needs to be global. Only save sections you'll reuse 3+ times. Unnecessary Global Sections clutter your library and slow decision-making.

3. Document Your Global Sections If you manage a team, keep a spreadsheet of your Global Sections, what they're used for, and when they were last updated. This prevents duplicates and confusion.

4. Version Your Sections If you have multiple versions of a section (dark footer, light footer), name them distinctly: "Footer – Dark" vs. "Footer – Light." This prevents mixing up variants.

5. Test Before Globalizing Build and test your section on one page first. Make sure it looks good, functions properly, and converts. Then save it globally. You don't want a broken design spreading across your entire account.

6. Archive Outdated Sections Over time, you'll create Global Sections you no longer use. Archive or delete old sections to keep your library clean and searchable. The new search feature makes this even more important—a cluttered library defeats its purpose.

Frequently Asked Questions

Can I use Global Sections in email templates?

Global Sections currently work in the page builder for websites, funnels, webinars, and e-commerce stores. For email sequences, you'll need to build sections directly within the email builder. However, you can copy the design and recreate it as a regular email section.

What happens if I delete a Global Section that's being used on multiple pages?

GoHighLevel will warn you before deletion and show you which pages are using that section. If you delete it anyway, the sections on those pages convert to regular sections, so you don't lose the design—they just stop updating globally. Always back up critical Global Sections before deletion.

Can I rename a Global Section after saving it?

Yes. Access your Global Section library, find the section you want to rename, and edit its name. The change applies immediately, and all pages using it still reference it correctly.

Are Global Sections included in different GoHighLevel plans?

Yes, Global Sections are available across all GoHighLevel plans (Standard, Professional, Agency, and Enterprise). The feature isn't limited to high-tier accounts, so even starter users can benefit from this time-saver.

Can I share Global Sections with team members or other accounts?

Global Sections are account-specific and can't be directly transferred between accounts. However, if you're in an Agency account, team members have access to all Global Sections within that account. For different accounts, you'd need to recreate the sections.

Global Sections are one of those features that sounds simple but delivers massive productivity gains once you start using them strategically. Whether you're a solo entrepreneur managing multiple funnels or an agency handling dozens of client projects, Global Sections cut your build time significantly and guarantee consistency across your entire digital presence.

The key is starting small—identify the 3-5 sections you build most frequently, create them as Global Sections, and expand from there. After a month, you'll wonder how you ever built without them.
